title: Align SPI clock mode and chip-select timing
description: Match clock polarity and phase to the peripheral and assert chip select with the timing margins the datasheet requires.
body: Use when SPI data reads back shifted, inverted, or unstable. Do not leave the controller at its default mode without checking the peripheral's required polarity and phase. Actions: read the datasheet for CPOL and CPHA, set the controller to match, and verify on a capture that data is sampled on the specified edge. Validation signals include a loopback or known-register read returning the expected value and chip select framing each transaction cleanly. Risk boundary: an off-by-one bit from a mode mismatch can silently corrupt configuration writes, so verify a read-back before trusting any write.
